title: Le futur proche — Near future & plans
objective: Say what you are going to do in the near future (A1: can make and describe simple plans).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: « going to » = aller + infinitive
bridge.body: The near future is easy: **aller** (present) + an **infinitive**. *Je vais manger* = “I’m going to eat”; *Nous allons voyager* = “We’re going to travel.” It mirrors English “going to + verb”. Hindi marks the future with verb endings (मैं खाऊँगा), so focus on the two-word French pattern: a form of *aller* + the base verb.

quiz.prompt: What is the main bridge idea for "Le futur proche — Near future & plans"?
options: Focus on: « going to » = aller + infinitive

example.french: Ce week-end, on va à Goa.
example.englishGloss: This weekend, we're going to Goa.

glosses.en: This weekend, we're going to Goa.
glosses.ta: இந்த வார இறுதியில், நாங்கள் Goa போகிறோம்.

example.french: La semaine prochaine, je vais visiter Agra.
example.englishGloss: Next week, I'm going to visit Agra.

glosses.en: Next week, I'm going to visit Agra.
glosses.ta: அடுத்த வாரம், நான் Agra பார்வையிடப் போகிறேன்.
glosses.kn: ಮುಂದಿನ ವಾರ, ನಾನು Agra ಭೇಟಿ ಮಾಡಲು ಹೋಗುತ್ತಿದ್ದೇನೆ.

example.englishGloss: We're going to travel by train.

glosses.en: We're going to travel by train.
